title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men are charged with kidnapping and assaulting a 28-year-old near Winter Park.
The suspects allegedly tied the victim with an extension cord and beat him with a wooden stake.
The men, identified as Calray Brown, Dijon Hazel, Newton Lawrence, and Alvin Barnett, are released on bail.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales and David Ardila were arrested by ICE at Logan Airport for immigration-related issues.
Rosales, from Florida, is detained despite her pending DACA renewal; Ardila, from Seattle, overstayed his visa.
ICE conducted target operations at Logan Airport, resulting in approximately 10 arrests over three months.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Five films are recommended to watch after 'The Odyssey,' exploring thematic and stylistic connections to Homer's epic.
'Interstellar,' 'Lawrence of Arabia,' and 'O Brother, Where Art Thou?' feature parallel narratives to Nolan's 'The Odyssey.'
Lighthearted choices include 'The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie,' inspired by Homer, and Western classic 'The Searchers.'

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox extended their win streak to 11 games after sweeping the Tampa Bay Rays in a doubleheader.
Game 1 saw the Red Sox win 10-0, with pitcher Jake Bennett allowing only one hit over six innings.
Boston's Game 2 victory, 5-3, was driven by Wilyer Abreu's two home runs and strong bullpen performance.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez could start on July 21 after a left groin strain recovery; Connelly Early's return date is still uncertain.
Kutter Crawford is throwing from 100 feet; Tanner Houck is throwing off a mound twice weekly post-Tommy John surgery.
Johan Oviedo to throw a bullpen session post-right elbow strain; no updates for Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet, others.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run and had three hits in the Red Sox's Game 1 win over the Rays.
Caleb Durbin went 5 for 7 across both doubleheader games, contributing effectively at the plate.
Jake Bennett pitched six scoreless innings with one hit, aiding the Red Sox's bullpen management.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots owner Robert Kraft voiced support for coach Mike Vrabel amidst controversy on ESPN's 'First Take.'
Vrabel denied an affair with NFL reporter Dianna Russini after photos sparked rumors; Russini later resigned.
Vrabel remains head coach after leading Patriots to the Super Bowl last year, starting training camp next week.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Lucky' starring Anya Taylor-Joy is now available on Apple TV, featuring a crime plot with stolen money.
'The Hunt for the Wilderpeople' featuring the late Sam Neill is streaming on Prime Video and Plex.
Curry Barker's horror film 'Obsession' has grossed over $400 million and is streaming on Peacock.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin, 25, was found guilty of violating New Hampshire's Civil Rights Act in a 2024 assault on a transgender woman.
The court ordered Lufkin to have no contact with the victim and imposed a $5,000 civil fine, $4,000 suspended for compliance.
Lufkin pleaded guilty to second-degree and simple assault, receiving a partially suspended 12-month sentence with a probationary period.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox are considering trade options as the Aug. 3 deadline approaches, ESPN's Jeff Passan reports.
Chief baseball officer Craig Breslow remains undecided on selling or adding players despite the team's recent win streak.
Boston's playoff odds have increased to 44.3% after a 10-game winning streak, emphasizing the importance of deadline decisions.
